title: Shinjis hallucination ep 4
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's hallucination (ep. #4)             Quote Post by belial11737 » Thu Sep 16, 2004 7:06 pm After Shinji leaves the movie theater he starts walking and everything starts spinning and there are these weird noices......why is he hallucinating? Is it because of guilt, shame? Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Sep 16, 2004 7:14 pm Shinji is still a child, out in the city at night by yourself is bound to make you paranoid.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Sep 16, 2004 10:26 pm It wasn't night. Maybe it was Eva reaching out to him. Or maybe it's just another, "Why did Rei appear" situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Fri Sep 17, 2004 3:39 pm ~There was problity somthing in the popcorn or somthing in the drinks. Mabye someone was smokeing pot in the thearter. ~on a more serious note it was probilty one of those things like the unheard line in EoE (gendos line)?

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Fri Sep 17, 2004 7:44 pm geez, do I gotta' answer everyt'ing 'round here? In ep. 4, when Shinji see's the buildings looming closer and hears voices, he's not hallucinating... ...the buildings were just actually coming alive and trying to eat the third child, that's all. :roll: see, sometimes the simplest answer is the most boring one- I mean...the right one.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Sep 18, 2004 9:47 pm Kiel wrote: geez, do I gotta' answer everyt'ing 'round here?

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Sun Sep 19, 2004 8:37 am Thanks Timesplitter, s'good to feel apriciated... ...I like makin' stuff up, makes me feel like a big man. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Sun Sep 19, 2004 9:23 am ill stick with my hypothsis

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat Sep 25, 2004 1:20 am i think it has to do with his feelings of oppression and guilt... as is also represented with the blood red sky i think...

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Sep 25, 2004 6:59 pm sadsadshinji wrote: i think it has to do with his feelings of oppression and guilt... as is also represented with the blood red sky i think...

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sat Sep 25, 2004 8:52 pm I also have a question about this scene. Why does everything suddenly turn pinkish red and then start spinning?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Sep 26, 2004 12:43 am he feels really guilty and unhappy....and has sufferend mental trauma... after all if you experienced an energy beam going through your head without it actually happening, thats gotta fry some nerve endings...

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Sep 26, 2004 6:44 am sadsadshinji wrote: he feels really guilty and unhappy....and has sufferend mental trauma... after all if you experienced an energy beam going through your head without it actually happening, thats gotta fry some nerve endings...

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Sep 26, 2004 4:58 pm that and the cicadas....lol...they really can screw with you (monotonus and neverending sounds)

[]:             Quote Post by Djeheuty » Sun Sep 26, 2004 5:23 pm does any know what the scenes after the 'hallucinations' mean. i know that one is a painting by Van Gogh and another looks like a painting by Caspar Friedrich, any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Sep 26, 2004 5:29 pm ummm...which set of hallucinations? and vangogh?

[]:             Quote Post by Djeheuty » Sun Sep 26, 2004 7:05 pm there not hallucinations but its when all the buildings are moving and the sky is red the artist Vincent Van Gogh

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Sep 26, 2004 8:50 pm i know the artist but there was some of his art during that scene? with the red sky?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Sun Sep 26, 2004 10:00 pm what epsoid was it in? :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Sun Sep 26, 2004 10:11 pm I think he's just scared of being alone in the dark. I get like that sometimes myself.
